ACME ANALYTICS INC.
INFORMATION SECURITY, PRIVACY, AND OPERATIONAL RESILIENCE POLICY
Document ID: ACME-ISP-2026-01
Version: 4.2
Effective Date: January 15, 2026
Last Reviewed: January 15, 2026
Next Review: January 15, 2027
Policy Owner: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
Approved By: Board of Directors, Audit Committee (Resolution 2026-01-05)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
This policy establishes the information security, data privacy, and
operational resilience requirements for Acme Analytics Inc. ("Acme") and
applies to all employees, contractors, systems, and customer data processed
by Acme's production SaaS platform ("the Service"). This policy is
reviewed annually by the CISO and approved by the Audit Committee of the
Board of Directors.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. GOVERNANCE AND EXECUTIVE ACCOUNTABILITY  (SOC 2 CC1.1, CC2.1, CC3.1)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
2.1 The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) reports directly to the
Chief Executive Officer and provides a quarterly security briefing to the
Board of Directors' Audit Committee. The Board has issued a formal
resolution (2026-01-05) affirming its commitment to information security
and allocating dedicated budget to the security program.

2.2 Acme maintains a documented Information Security Management System
(ISMS) aligned to ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria. All
security policies are reviewed and re-approved annually; the last review
was completed on January 15, 2026.

2.3 A formal enterprise risk assessment is conducted at least annually by
the Risk & Compliance team, with output recorded in the Acme Risk
Register (maintained in ServiceNow GRC). The most recent assessment was
completed on December 10, 2025 and reviewed by the Audit Committee on
January 8, 2026. The register tracks likelihood, impact, owner, and
mitigation status for each identified risk.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. LOGICAL ACCESS CONTROLS  (SOC 2 CC6.1, CC6.2, CC6.3)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.1 All access to production systems and customer data is controlled
through Okta Single Sign-On with role-based access control (RBAC). Access
is granted based on the principle of least privilege and requires
documented manager approval via a ServiceNow access request ticket.

3.2 Multi-factor authentication (MFA) using Duo Security is mandatory for:
    (a) all VPN connections into the production environment,
    (b) all privileged/administrative accounts (including root, database
        admin, and cloud console access),
    (c) all remote access to internal management consoles.
MFA cannot be bypassed; exceptions require written CISO approval and are
reviewed monthly.

3.3 User provisioning and deprovisioning follow a documented joiner-mover-
leaver process owned by IT Operations and integrated with Workday HR.
Terminated employees have all access revoked within 4 hours of the HR
termination event; emergency terminations are handled within 1 hour.
Access reviews are performed quarterly by system owners; evidence of the
most recent review (Q1 2026) is retained in the Access Review SharePoint.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. SECURITY MONITORING AND INCIDENT RESPONSE  (SOC 2 CC7.1, CC7.2)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
4.1 Acme operates a 24x7 Security Operations Center (SOC) using Splunk
Enterprise Security as the SIEM platform. All production systems, cloud
control planes (AWS CloudTrail, GuardDuty), and endpoint telemetry
(CrowdStrike Falcon) are ingested into Splunk for correlation and
alerting.

4.2 The Acme Incident Response Plan (IRP-v3, dated November 2025) defines
severity tiers with the following SLAs:
    - P1 (critical): 1-hour detection, 4-hour containment, 24-hour customer notification
    - P2 (high): 4-hour detection, 24-hour containment
    - P3 (medium): 1-business-day detection, 5-business-day containment
The IR plan is tested semi-annually via tabletop exercise; the most recent
exercise was conducted on October 14, 2025 and findings are tracked to
closure in JIRA project IR-EX.

4.3 External penetration tests are performed annually by an independent
third party (NCC Group). The most recent engagement concluded on
September 22, 2025 with the final report issued October 3, 2025. All
high- and critical-severity findings from that test have been remediated,
tracked in the NCC-2025-REM JIRA project, with evidence of closure.

4.4 Internal vulnerability scans are run weekly using Tenable Nessus
against the production environment. Critical vulnerabilities must be
remediated within 14 days; high severity within 30 days.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. CHANGE MANAGEMENT  (SOC 2 CC8.1)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
5.1 All changes to production systems follow the Acme Change Management
Policy (CM-POL-02). Changes must be: (a) raised as a ticket in JIRA, (b)
peer-reviewed through a GitHub pull request with at least one approving
reviewer, (c) tested in the staging environment, and (d) approved by the
Change Advisory Board (CAB) for standard changes, or by the on-call
engineering manager for emergency changes.

5.2 Segregation of duties is enforced technically: developers cannot
deploy to production. Deployments are executed by the Platform
Engineering team through an automated CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions +
Terraform Cloud) with the deployment identity separate from developer
identities.

5.3 Emergency changes are logged, labeled "EMERGENCY" in JIRA, and
reviewed within 5 business days by the CAB with post-implementation
review documented in the ticket.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. DATA CLASSIFICATION AND CONFIDENTIALITY  (SOC 2 C1.1, C1.2)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
6.1 Acme classifies data into four tiers: PUBLIC, INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL,
and RESTRICTED. Customer data is by default classified as CONFIDENTIAL;
personal data and authentication credentials are classified RESTRICTED.
The full classification scheme is defined in the Acme Data Classification
Standard (DCS-v2, March 2026) and the data inventory is maintained in
OneTrust.

6.2 All CONFIDENTIAL and RESTRICTED data is encrypted in transit using
TLS 1.2 or higher (TLS 1.3 preferred) and at rest using AES-256 via AWS
KMS with customer-managed keys (CMKs) where contractually required. Key
rotation occurs annually and is logged in CloudTrail.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. PRIVACY  (SOC 2 P3.1, P4.1, P6.1)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
7.1 Acme's Privacy Notice is published at https://acme.example.com/privacy
and discloses the categories of personal data collected, purposes of
processing, lawful bases, retention periods, and data subject rights. The
notice was last reviewed on January 20, 2026.

7.2 Data subject access requests (DSARs) are handled by the Privacy
Office and tracked in OneTrust DSAR module. Acme honors access,
rectification, deletion, and portability rights within the statutory
timelines (30 days under GDPR, 45 days under CCPA).

7.3 All third-party sub-processors that handle personal data on Acme's
behalf are subject to a written Data Processing Agreement (DPA) and a
pre-engagement privacy and security assessment performed by the Privacy
Office. The current sub-processor list is published at
https://acme.example.com/subprocessors and re-assessed annually.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
8. AVAILABILITY, BUSINESS CONTINUITY, AND DISASTER RECOVERY  (SOC 2 A1.1, A1.2, A1.3)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
8.1 Acme's standard Customer SLA (CS-SLA-2026, March 2026) guarantees
99.9% monthly uptime for the production Service, with service credits for
downtime beyond that threshold. Uptime is measured and published monthly
on the Acme Trust Portal.

8.2 Acme maintains a documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP-v5, dated
February 2026) and a Disaster Recovery Plan (DR-v5, dated February 2026).
The DR plan defines an RTO of 4 hours and an RPO of 1 hour for the
production Service.

8.3 The DR plan is tested at least annually via a full failover exercise
to the secondary AWS region (us-west-2). The most recent DR test was
completed on February 18, 2026; all test objectives were met and the
results report (DR-TEST-2026-01) was signed off by the VP of Engineering.

8.4 System capacity is monitored continuously via Datadog dashboards with
automated alerting on CPU, memory, database connections, and queue depth.
Capacity reviews are performed monthly by the SRE team against a 12-month
forecast.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
9. CERTIFICATIONS AND THIRD-PARTY AUDITS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
- SOC 2 Type II: issued by KPMG, report period 2025-01-01 to 2025-12-31,
  clean opinion, report available under NDA.
- ISO 27001:2022: certified by BSI, certificate number IS 745123, valid
  through 2027-08-14.
- PCI DSS: not applicable (Acme does not store cardholder data).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
10. POLICY REVIEW AND ATTESTATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
This policy is reviewed annually by the CISO, approved by the Audit
Committee, and re-published to all personnel. All employees complete
annual security awareness training and attest to this policy during
onboarding and at each annual review cycle.

                             - END OF POLICY -
